summary: Removed fighter-character-specific `HSD_GObj` typedef aliases such as `ftKb_GObj`, `ftSs_GObj`, and `ftYs_GObj`, replacing affected function signatures, callback typedefs, struct fields, headers, and generated `.dox` declarations with the shared `Fighter_GObj*` type. The decomp.dev report said GALE01 had no changes, indicating this was a type/header cleanup with no codegen impact.
what_changed: Deleted fighter-specific `HSD_GObj` forward typedefs from Kirby, Samus, and Yoshi forward headers: `ftKb_GObj`, `ftSs_GObj`, and `ftYs_GObj`.
Replaced many Kirby signatures in `ftKb_Init.c`, `ftKb_Init.h`, `ftKb_Init.dox`, and `ftKb_Init.static.h` from `ftKb_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*`.
Updated `ftCo_ThrownKirby` declarations and local function pointer typedefs so Kirby throw/copy-star helpers use `Fighter_GObj*` instead of `ftKb_GObj*` for thrower or Kirby gobj parameters.
Propagated equivalent alias removal through Yoshi guard/special-N and Samus special-N headers and implementations.
Adjusted item-side interfaces touching Kirby/Yoshi/Samus interactions, including `it_2F2B`, `itkirbyhammer`, and `itkirbyyoshispecialn`, to match the shared fighter object type.
The diff is mostly one-for-one signature and documentation churn: 2049 additions and 2052 deletions across 21 files.
smart_moves: Standardized on the already meaningful shared alias `Fighter_GObj*` instead of preserving per-character aliases that all pointed at `struct HSD_GObj`.
Removed the aliases at their source in `forward.h` rather than only changing call sites, preventing new code from continuing to use `ftKb_GObj`, `ftSs_GObj`, or `ftYs_GObj`.
Updated implementation files, public headers, static headers, and `.dox` declarations together, reducing the chance of prototype drift.
Kept the cleanup codegen-neutral; the decomp.dev GALE01 report explicitly recorded `No changes`.
Preserved more specific fighter data aliases such as `ftKb_Fighter` and `ftKb_DatAttrs` where they describe actual character-specific data, while eliminating only the object wrapper aliases.
decomp_lessons: Do not introduce or keep fighter-specific `GObj` typedefs when the underlying object type is not character-specific; use `Fighter_GObj*` for fighter game-object parameters.
A type-alias cleanup can touch many prototypes without affecting matching, but it must be propagated through C files, headers, static headers, generated docs, and cross-system item interfaces.
Shared motion-variable fields should use the broadest truthful type; `capturekirby.thrower_gobj` is a fighter object, not specifically a Kirby-only object.
When replacing aliases in decomp code, include local callback typedefs as well as function declarations; otherwise stale aliases can survive in indirect-call helper types.
Use automated codegen reports to validate large mechanical type churn; here the bot reported no GALE01 changes.
naming_conventions: Use `Fighter_GObj*` for generic fighter object parameters.
Avoid character-prefixed aliases like `ftKb_GObj`, `ftSs_GObj`, and `ftYs_GObj` when they are only aliases for `HSD_GObj`.
Keep character-specific prefixes for real character data structs, e.g. `ftKb_Fighter` and `ftKb_DatAttrs`, when the evidence shows they remain distinct.
Parameter names such as `thrower_gobj`, `victim_gobj`, and `gobj` can remain semantically descriptive even when the type becomes `Fighter_GObj*`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Large-scale prototype-only refactors should be checked against decomp.dev/GALE01 for no assembly changes.
Changing a typedef alias from `ftKb_GObj*` to `Fighter_GObj*` is source-level type cleanup and should not alter codegen when the pointer representation and accessed fields are unchanged.
Update callback typedefs such as `KirbyVelocityFunc` and `GetFloatFunc` in the same pass as the functions they describe to avoid mismatched indirect-call signatures.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The only visible issue comment was from `decomp-dev[bot]`: `Report for GALE01 ... No changes`.
follow_up_queries: Search for remaining fighter-specific aliases matching `ft*_GObj` that typedef `struct HSD_GObj`.
Check whether other character forward headers still define redundant `HSD_GObj` aliases.
Audit item and fighter callback typedefs for stale character-specific object aliases.
Review generated `.dox` files when changing prototypes so documentation stays synchronized.
